policy_statement: The City of Geneva intends to reduce greenhouse gas emissions by 60% by 2030 and achieve carbon neutrality by 2050.
verbatim_text: The City of Geneva declared a climate emergency in February 2020. It intends to reduce greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions by 60% by 2030 and to achieve carbon neutrality by 2050, in order to limit climate change and comply with the Paris Agreement.
section_header: A WORD FROM THE EXECUTIVE COUNCIL
extraction_rationale: Contains quantifiable targets with deadlines (60% by 2030; carbon neutrality by 2050) stated as an intention by the municipal government.

policy_statement: The City of Geneva will incorporate climate change considerations into all its public policies through its Climate Strategy.
verbatim_text: The municipal's Climate Strategy marks a new stage in its actions, by incorporating climate change into all its public policies.
section_header: A WORD FROM THE EXECUTIVE COUNCIL
extraction_rationale: A stated governance commitment to mainstream climate across municipal public policies (specific intervention: incorporation into all policies).

policy_statement: The City of Geneva will implement strong measures to transform consumption patterns, with short supply chains and the circular economy central to its actions.
verbatim_text: The City of Geneva's aim is to 'consume less and better'. The city will therefore be implementing strong measures to support and speed up the transformation of our current modes of consumption to alternative modes that must be more desirable, practical and affordable than they are today. Short supply chains and the circular economy will be central to its actions.
section_header: Priority 1 CONSUMPTION OF GOODS AND SERVICES
extraction_rationale: Uses binding forward-looking language (“will be implementing”) and names specific intervention areas (short supply chains, circular economy).

policy_statement: The City of Geneva will strengthen cooperation with Greater Geneva stakeholders to ensure a supply of high-quality food accessible to all, supporting a healthier, more local diet with less emphasis on meat.
verbatim_text: One of the main objectives under this heading is a healthy, local diet with less emphasis on meat. The City will strengthen its cooperation with the various key players in Greater Geneva to ensure a supply of high-quality food that is accessible to all.
section_header: Priority 1 CONSUMPTION OF GOODS AND SERVICES
extraction_rationale: Concrete commitment (“will strengthen its cooperation”) tied to a defined outcome (food supply that is high-quality and accessible).

policy_statement: The City of Geneva will ensure that sustainable resource-use principles (sharing, recycling and circular economy) are applied across culture, sports and recreation.
verbatim_text:
As a result, the City has chosen to act mainly on modes of consumption by supporting the sustainable use of resources, sharing, recycling and the circular economy more generally.

The City will ensure that these principles are applied across the board in culture, sports and recreation, while guaranteeing that multiple dynamic and diverse activities remain on offer.
section_header: Priority 1 CONSUMPTION OF GOODS AND SERVICES
extraction_rationale: Explicit municipal action commitment (“will ensure”) specifying intervention approach and application domains.

policy_statement: The City of Geneva aims to heat buildings using 100% renewable sources by 2050.
verbatim_text: The challenge we have set is to heat buildings using 100% renewable sources by 2050.
section_header: CLIMATE TARGETS AT THE HEART OF OUR ENERGY POLICY
extraction_rationale: Quantified target with deadline (100% renewable heating by 2050) stated as a defined challenge/target.

policy_statement: The City of Geneva will act to reduce space reserved for cars and motorcycles in order to reduce motorised traffic and limit its negative impacts.
verbatim_text: The City will act to reduce the space currently reserved for cars and motorcycles to reduce motorised traffic and limit its negative impacts.
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention in urban transport management (“reduce the space reserved”) stated with “will act”.

policy_statement: The City of Geneva will make parts of the urban area more permeable and create new interconnected planted areas with trees and water.
verbatim_text: The City of Geneva will embark on a process of making part of the urban area more permeable and will create a series of new, interconnected planted areas with trees and water.
section_header: A NETWORK OF OASES IN THE CITY
extraction_rationale: Clear planned interventions for climate resilience/adaptation (permeability; green/blue infrastructure) expressed with “will”.

policy_statement: The City of Geneva will strengthen actions to protect its most vulnerable citizens as part of its commitment to equality.
verbatim_text: The City will strengthen its actions to protect its most vulnerable citizens - children, young people, older people and those in precarious situations - as part of its commitment to equality.
section_header: CLIMATE, HEALTH AND QUALITY OF LIFE: A COMMITMENT TO ALL
extraction_rationale: Concrete commitment (“will strengthen its actions”) specifying target groups (vulnerable populations).

policy_statement: The City of Geneva plans to restore, protect and develop its natural ecosystems and take practical actions to protect the soil.
verbatim_text: The City of Geneva plans to restore, protect and develop its natural ecosystems. In addition, it will take practical actions to protect the soil
section_header: PROTECTING BIODIVERSITY IN THE HEART OF THE CITY
extraction_rationale: Stated planned actions for ecosystem restoration/protection and soil protection; despite being broad, it includes specific intervention areas.

policy_statement: The City of Geneva will provide citizens with practical tools to inform them, encourage collective action, and support behavioural changes that lower personal climate impact.
verbatim_text: The City of Geneva will provide its citizens with practical tools to keep them informed, encourage them to take collective action and to support behavioural changes which lower their personal climat impact.
extraction_rationale: Explicit commitment (“will provide”) with specific intervention type (practical tools) and intended outcomes (collective action/behaviour change).

policy_statement: The City of Geneva will work with key financial-centre players to support sustainable finance, including improving transparency in calculating financial sector emissions, and will align its own investments and holdings with climate targets.
verbatim_text: The City of Geneva will work alongside the key players in the financial centre to support sustainable finance and encourage them to get involved, in particular to ensure greater transparency in calculating the financial sector's emissions. It will ensure its own investments and financial holdings are compatible with climate targets to lead the way.
extraction_rationale: Multiple clear commitments using “will” and “will ensure,” including a concrete governance/finance alignment action (city investments compatible with climate targets).
